title: How the fuck did I miss this
display_title: How the fuck did I miss this
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Aug 21, 2008 12:57 am Seen that in a Facebook group, and they indicated that the battle scene shown there is a mirror image of the original, so it is slightly incorrect. Though the same body motions are used by Sachiel and Misato, which is pretty cool. Symbolism at its finest.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:01 am Wow, no wonder Shinji is uncomfortable at Misato's.

[]:             Quote Post by swamp147 » Thu Aug 21, 2008 1:58 am I love NGE so much; there are so many little quirks and parallels that are always being discovered (even if it is only me who is seeing it for the first time). Thanks for sharing!

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 21, 2008 3:18 am Old news is old. Still a very cool little echo though.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Thu Aug 21, 2008 6:54 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: Old news is old.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 21, 2008 7:55 am Shin-seiki wrote: Of course, if you wanna play 'spot the visual self-references in NGE', we could do that all day:

[]:             Quote Post by Barinax » Thu Aug 21, 2008 6:10 pm Wow, I just spent an hour or so on that site. I'd like to blame you but I get very easily distracted anyway...

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Thu Aug 21, 2008 7:16 pm

[]:             Quote Post by Caiden » Thu Aug 21, 2008 8:57 pm lol thats pretty neat.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Aug 22, 2008 9:21 am I think it is either intentional, just a little play around by Gainax, or they basically used the same movements because they got better better use of their imagination somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 27, 2008 7:01 am The Imperialist wrote: I think it is either intentional, just a little play around by Gainax, or they basically used the same movements because they got better better use of their imagination somewhere else.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:37 pm Well, as you said, if it is intentional, well, that will back up that Eva has a bit of political message in it. In my previous post, I was just putting up all the logical possibilities.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Aug 27, 2008 12:52 pm Given Anno's developed visual style on display through NGE and his subsequent live film Love and Pop I'd most definitely argue that it's intentional. There are numerous references and styles taken from past directors (like Kubrick) who used these techniques extensively and Anno certainly has the visual knowledge and awareness for such visual storytelling. Also, I thought it was widely known NGE was political. I've heard (though just heard) that it's viewed as more of a political work in Japan contrasted with the Western view of it being an existentialist piece.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Thu Aug 28, 2008 7:57 am It is true. We do kind of see it as kind of a political message/protest at the present social conditions etc. That was what I was kind of saying in the thread of Perception differences between Japan and the West, but many people just struck down the idea that it is a political message carrier. And if we look at the history of anime, it either makes a moral point or a political protest.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Thu Aug 28, 2008 9:15 am Shin-seiki wrote: Eva Yojimbo wrote: Old news is old.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Aug 28, 2008 10:21 pm The Imperialist wrote: And if we look at the history of anime, it either makes a moral point or a political protest.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Aug 29, 2008 4:13 am PRECISELY MY CHAP! The notion of Japan being a nation of children, mind you, was not addressed just by Anno. It is also addressed by Kobayashi Yoshinori, and a few other critics of politics and society. However, it is just my hunch, but we may have a bit more political protest aside from Japan being immature, both generally, and politically.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaiku » Fri Sep 05, 2008 5:54 am The Imperialist wrote: PRECISELY MY CHAP! The notion of Japan being a nation of children, mind you, was not addressed just by Anno. It is also addressed by Kobayashi Yoshinori, and a few other critics of politics and society. However, it is just my hunch, but we may have a bit more political protest aside from Japan being immature, both generally, and politically.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Fri Sep 05, 2008 7:10 am No, they mean that Japan has become both socially, and politically immature.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Fri Sep 05, 2008 12:55 pm Shin-seiki wrote:
